summary: Git push to create a pull request failed with a 403 Forbidden error. The `nextjs-bot` user lacks permission to push to `vercel/next.js`, causing the sync-react workflow to abort after successfully building the vendored React files.
root_cause: The GitHub token or bot credentials used for `git push origin update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422` lack sufficient permissions. The error `remote: Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot` and `fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/': The requested URL returned error: 403` indicate the bot account cannot push to the repository.

evidence.quote: remote: Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot.
evidence.reason: Explicit permission denial from GitHub for the nextjs-bot user attempting to push

evidence.quote: fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/': The requested URL returned error: 403
evidence.reason: HTTP 403 Forbidden error confirms authentication/authorization failure when attempting git push

evidence.quote: Command failed with exit code 128 (Unknown system error -128): git push origin update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422
evidence.reason: The final command in the sync-react script that fails is the git push operation

suggested_fix: Verify that the GitHub token provided to the workflow has the correct permissions (Contents: write is shown but may be insufficient for this user) or ensure the `nextjs-bot` account has push access to the `vercel/next.js` repository. Consider checking if the bot's permissions were revoked or if an organization-level protection rule is blocking the push.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
